news.t: German Factory Orders Fell Back in April
German Factory Orders Jumped in First Month of Iran Conflict

holdings.infoBox: FLGR is an equity ETF with a total of 68 individual holdings. The top holdings are Siemens Aktiengesellschaft stock at 11.71%, Allianz SE at 8.87%, SAP SE at 8.69%, Siemens Energy AG at 6.87%, and Infineon Technologies AG at 6.27%.

overview.description: The Franklin FTSE Germany ETF (FLGR) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the FTSE Germany RIC Capped index. The fund offers market cap-based exposure to large- and mid-cap German equities. FLGR was launched on Nov 2, 2017 and is issued by Franklin Templeton.

data.title: German Factory Orders Fell Back in April
data.text: German manufacturing orders dropped in April, reversing some of the gains in March that came on the back of stock building after the outbreak of the war in Iran.

data.title: German Factory Orders Jumped in First Month of Iran Conflict
data.text: Orders rose sharply in March, a signal of potential front-running to manage rising energy prices and supply disruptions that arose after the start of the war in Iran.

data.title: Strategist makes the case for investing in Germany and China
data.text: The potential impact of tariffs on the US equity market (^GSPC, ^IXIC, ^DJI) could be more significant than expected. Alastair Pinder, HSBC head of emerging markets and global equity strategist, joins...
